archived_events.message: New scenario: The Road to Karendel. Traveling with merchant Hallan and Mira on the trade road.
[GM] [Load] The Road to Karendel — Chapter 1: Trade Road. Traveling with merchant Hallan and Mira.
[GM] [Load] The Road to Karendel — Trade road, traveling with merchant Hallan/Mira.
[GM] [Load] The Road to Karendel — Trade road, traveling with merchant Hallan/Mira.
[GM] Departure preparations complete. Cart repaired. Hallan's leg injury slows travel speed.
archived_events.narrative: The cart wheel repair was finished. Hallan limped onto the cart. Mira organized the luggage and looked at the party. "We can leave now. About a day to Karendel... it'll be a bit slow because of father's leg." The road continues east along the trade route. The sun is still high.

archived_events.message: [GM] Departure preparation. Saiki suggests to Noel to pick up items dropped on the ground. Hallan and Mira react.
archived_events.narrative: Hallan turned from the cart. "That stuff? Got trampled by wolves, so it's worthless as merchandise. Take it if you want." He paused, then added, "...The actually useful stuff is already on the cart. Thanks for the help." Mira rummaged through her bag and pulled out a small vial, handing it to Noel. "I brought this to treat father, but there's one left. It's a thank-you for your help." Noel nodded as he took the vial. Scattered on the ground were torn cloth scraps, broken pottery shards, and a few handfuls of crushed dried fruit. Hardly anything useful, but as Noel searched thoroughly, he found a coil of rope and a flint.

archived_events.message: [GM] [Test] Pack horse portrait regeneration
[GM] [Regeneration] Pack horse portrait

[GM] [Regeneration] Pack horse portrait (background removal test)
[GM] [Regeneration] Pack horse portrait (background removal test)
[GM] The party boards the cart and departs along the trade road toward Karendel.
archived_events.narrative:
Hallan took the reins and climbed onto the cart. He carefully folded his injured left leg and settled in, then patted the pack horse's back once. The horse snorted and began plodding forward.

Mira organized the luggage from the back of the cart and said cheerfully,
"If we stay the night at the crossroads rest stop, we'll arrive at Karendel by tomorrow afternoon. I think it's best to camp at the rest stop before sunset."

Hallan nodded briefly.
"...With good winds, we'll get there by tomorrow afternoon."

Oak forests stretched along both sides of the trade road. Wind rustled the leaves, and birdsong could be heard. The cart wheels rattled along the dirt path, moving forward. It was a peaceful afternoon.

archived_events.message: [GM] Wolf carcass butchered. Meat and hide given to Hallan.
archived_events.narrative: Noel skillfully butchered the wolf carcass. A hide and some cuts of meat were produced. Hallan nodded and accepted them. "Thanks. Should fetch a few coins at Karendel." Mira wrapped the meat in cloth and loaded it onto the cart. "Grilled meat for dinner tonight!"

archived_events.message: [GM] Saiki switches seats with Lucena.
archived_events.narrative: Lucena grinned as she moved to the new seat. "What, suddenly? Is the view better on that side?"

archived_events.message: [GM] Saiki says "I have something to experiment with" and switches seats with Lucena again.
archived_events.narrative:
Saiki spoke to Lucena. "I have something to experiment with." Lucena blinked and moved aside. "Again? What is it, tell me."

After switching seats on the cart, Saiki ended up sitting next to Mira. Mira tilted her head curiously. "An experiment? Something magic-related?"

Hallan glanced back while holding the reins. "...You're not going to blow something up on the cart, are you?"

archived_events.message: [GM] Saiki asked about the distance to Karendel. Hallan explains the terrain.
archived_events.narrative:
Saiki looked around. Oak forests still stretched along both sides of the trade road.

Hallan gestured with his chin toward a signpost by the road while holding the reins.
"...See that fork up ahead? That's the halfway point. Head south from there and you reach the port; keep going east and it's Karendel."

Mira looked up at the sky and said,
"Once the sun drops a bit more, we'll arrive at the crossroads rest stop. We'll stay the night there, and by tomorrow afternoon, we'll see Karendel's gates."

Hallan added briefly,
"...As long as we don't run into bandits or beasts."

archived_events.message: [GM] Saiki asked about directions. Hallan explains the crossroads geography in detail.
archived_events.narrative:
Hallan nodded while gripping the reins.
"That's roughly right. This trade road goes straight east and then turns at the crossroads. From there, go left — that is, north — and you reach Karendel."

Mira chimed in.
"The south road goes down toward the coast. There's a port town there... what was its name again."

Hallan answered briefly.
"...Seren. A small fishing harbor. You can take a ship from there along the coast to western cities, but we have no business there."

The cart rattled forward. Ahead on the trade road, the point where the path splits into two was faintly coming into view.

archived_events.message: [GM] Saiki takes a short rest on the cart. MP 37→40 recovered.
archived_events.narrative:
Saiki pulled his robe tighter and leaned against one side of the cart. Closing his eyes and steadying his breath, the faintly scattered mana began to slowly gather back into his body.

Mira glanced sideways at him briefly, then turned her gaze forward.

Lucena sat on the edge of the cart with her legs dangling, chewing on a fruit.
"Don't fall off while you sleep. Old man Hallan won't stop for you."

Hallan snorted without looking back.
"...If you fall, you'll wake up."

The cart slowly continued east along the trade road. Wind rustled the leaves, and the afternoon sun was warm.

archived_events.message: [GM] Saiki asked about stopping at the crossroads rest stop. Hallan explains practical reasons.
archived_events.narrative:
Hallan thought for a moment while gripping the reins, then spoke.
"...I understand the hurry, but there are reasons."

Hallan counted on his fingers.
"First, the pack horse is tired. We came all the way from Harun without stopping. If the horse collapses, the cart stops too."
"Second, the road from the crossroads to Karendel has mountain paths mixed in. If a wheel gets stuck in the dark, we're done for."
"Third..." Hallan lowered his voice. "Merchants always gather at the crossroads rest stop. If there's a group heading to Karendel together, it's safer."

Mira chimed in.
"And the rest stop has a well. We need to refill the water barrel too."

Hallan added briefly.
"...Of course, it's your call. If you want to travel through the night, I won't stop you, but I wouldn't recommend it."

archived_events.message: [GM] Crossroads rest stop camp (night). Lucena returns from scouting. Noel stands guard.
archived_events.narrative:
Lucena slipped away from the campfire and melted into the darkness. She circled the rest stop, checking the travelers' belongings, but could only make out silhouettes in the dark. When she passed near the peddler's bundle, she felt a gaze from the swordsman's direction. She paused for a moment, then casually turned back.

Returning to the campfire, Lucena scooted up next to Saiki and whispered softly.
"I checked everything out~ The peddler's lock is toy-level, and the sword guy is the type who won't let go of his blade even in his sleep. But that hooded person over there... has zero luggage. Isn't that suspicious?"

Noel silently added a log to the fire. The flames leapt up and brightened the surroundings. With his axe resting on his knees, he'd been scanning the darkness, then placed a blanket on Saiki.
"...Cover up."

Beyond the campfire, the hooded figure's shadow sat motionless. The swordsman was still in the same posture, sipping from his cup. The night was deepening.

dialogues.speaker: lucena
dialogues.line: I checked everything out~ The peddler's lock is toy-level, and the sword guy is the type who won't let go of his blade even in his sleep. But that hooded person over there... has zero luggage. Isn't that suspicious?

dialogues.speaker: noel
dialogues.line: ...Cover up.

dice_rolls.context: Stealth scouting - slightly noticed by the swordsman
Cargo appraisal - failed to assess value in the dark

archived_events.message: [GM] Mana wire cast + night watch rotation set (1st shift Lucena, 2nd shift Noel). Camp for the night.
archived_events.narrative:
Saiki draped the blanket over his shoulders and said,
"If we want to wake up early tomorrow, we should sleep early."

Hallan cut in briefly.
"...We need to set a watch. There are strangers mixed in here."

Saiki nodded, then pulled out his staff and slowly traced a circle on the ground around the camp. A thin thread of mana unwound from the tip of the staff and seeped between the blades of grass. An invisible detection line settled into place around the campsite.

Lucena's eyes sparkled as she asked.
"Oh, what's that? A trap?"

"Mana wire. If someone crosses it, I'll sense it."

Lucena nodded, then twirled a dagger between her fingers and settled by the campfire.
"Alright, but we still need a watch. That hooded person earlier was really suspicious. I'm not sleepy yet, so I'll take the first shift."

Noel replied briefly.
"...Second shift."

He glanced at Saiki and nodded once — meaning, get some good sleep.

Saiki pulled the blanket over himself and lay down by the campfire. He could faintly sense the mana threads surrounding the area. Lucena added another log to the fire. Flames rose and warmed the night air.

Deep in the night. An owl's cry echoed from the forest.

dialogues.line: If we want to wake up early tomorrow, we should sleep early.

dialogues.speaker: hallan

dialogues.speaker: lucena

dialogues.speaker: noel
dialogues.line: ...Second shift.

archived_events.message: [GM] Uneventful night. Morning wake-up. Long rest: all members HP/MP full.
archived_events.narrative:
The night was quiet.

The mana wire vibrated faintly a couple of times, but it was only small wild animals passing through. The campfire gradually died down, and stars filled the sky.

At shift change, Lucena approached Noel and whispered softly.
"That hooded one was only pretending to sleep. Tell Saiki too."
Noel nodded and gripped his axe. Lucena clutched her daggers to her chest and fell into a light sleep.

At dawn. Noel detected movement in the darkness. The hooded figure quietly rose and slipped out of the rest stop, returning shortly after. When they returned, he could see them wiping their hands on their clothes.

Before long, dawn broke.

With birdsong and morning sunlight illuminating the rest stop, Saiki opened his eyes. The mana wire's sensation had faded — its effect had expired. His body felt light. A full night's sleep had completely restored his mana.

Noel looked toward Saiki and said briefly.
"...The hooded one went out once at dawn."

Looking around, the hooded figure's spot was empty. No one knew when they had left. The swordsman was still leaning against the wall with his sword in his arms, and the peddler was packing up and preparing to depart.

Hallan said while watering the pack horse.
"...We can leave in about an hour. We'll reach Karendel today."

dialogues.speaker: lucena
dialogues.line: That hooded one was only pretending to sleep. Tell Saiki too.

dialogues.speaker: noel
dialogues.line: ...The hooded one went out once at dawn.

dialogues.speaker: hallan
dialogues.line: ...We can leave in about an hour. We'll reach Karendel today.

archived_events.message: [GM] Peddler/swordsman join as travel companions. Departure toward Karendel.
archived_events.narrative:
Saiki approached the peddler packing his things and the swordsman leaning against the wall.

"We're heading to Karendel. If you're going the same way, would you like to travel together?"

The peddler frantically gathered his bundle and nodded repeatedly.
"Oh my, I was just worried about going alone! If you'd join me, nothing could be more welcome..."

The swordsman, still in his position leaning against the wall with his hand on his sword, regarded Saiki for a moment.
"...Karendel."
After a brief silence, he slowly stood up.
"I happen to have business that way. We merely share the same road, so don't mind me."

He glanced at the empty spot where the hooded figure had been, but said nothing.

Hallan said while hitching the pack horse.
"...No reason to object. The more of us there are, the less beasts will bother us."

Lucena sidled up to Saiki and whispered.
"The peddler is whatever, but that swordsman... he was really good at muffling his footsteps while pretending to sleep. He might not be a thief, but he's not ordinary either."

Noel chimed in while slinging his axe over his shoulder.
"Having one more guy who can use a blade makes things a bit easier for me."

The party had grown. Hallan patted the pack horse's back, and the cart began to move slowly. Morning mist lay thin over the trade road. Half a day or so until Karendel.

archived_events.message: [GM] Turn 24: 할란에게 카렌델까지 얼마나 걸리냐고 묻는다
archived_events.narrative:
할란은 카렌델까지의 거리에 대한 질문에 잠시 생각을 하더니 말합니다. 그는 손가락을 이용해 거리를 계산하고, 고개를 끄덕입니다.

루세나가 할란에게 카렌델까지의 여정에 대해 묻습니다. 여행에 필요한 정보를 얻기 위해 할란에게 질문하는 것이 합리적입니다.

dialogues.speaker: hallan
dialogues.line: 카렌델까지는 반나절 정도면 도착할 수 있습니다. 오늘 오후에 도착할 수 있으면 좋겠네요.
